data.title: OneSpaWorld Non-GAAP EPS of $0.24 misses by $0.02, revenue of $242.1M misses by $0.7M
data.description:
• OneSpaWorld press release (OSW): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.24 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $242.1M (+11.5% Y/Y) misses by $0.7M.
  • Total revenues increased 11% to $242.1 million compared to $217.2 million for the fourth quarter of 2024, driven by fleet expansion from 2025 new ship builds, a 2% increase in revenue days, and a 1% increase in average guest spend, contributing $15.5 million, $8.7 million and $2.1 million, respectively, to the increase in Total revenues, of which $2.8 million was attributable to increased guest pre-booked services. Growth in our Maritime Total revenues was offset by a $1.3 million decrease in destination resorts Total revenues, partially due to the closure of hotels where we had previously operated.

  • Fiscal Year 2026 Guidance

     

     

     

    Three Months Ended March 31, 2026

     

     

    Year Ended December 31, 2026

    Total Revenues

     

    $

    241-246 million

     

    $

    1.01-1.03 billion

    Adjusted EBITDA

     

    $

    30-32 million

     

    $

    128-138 million

data.title: Hackett Group outlines Q1 2026 revenue guidance of $70.5M-$72M amid full GenAI platform rollout and new partnership initiatives
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: The Hackett Group (HCKT) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Ted Fernandez, Co-Founder, Chairman & CEO, reported that "we reported revenues before reimbursements of $74.8 million and adjusted earnings per share of $0.40, which were above and at the high of our quarterly guidance, respectively." Fernandez emphasized the systematic expansion of GenAI-enabled platforms, asserting, "we have been systematically expanding our suite of GenAI-enabled platforms to lead in the rapidly emerging Agentic enterprise era."
  • Fernandez described the launch of AI XPLR version 5 and highlighted its licensable status, stating, "AI XPLR is distinct due to its enterprise-wide solution simulation, ideation and detailed process and agentic design capabilities, which are supported by solution-specific ROI."
  • The company announced a full suite of GenAI-enabled platforms supporting nearly all services, including XT, AIX, and Ask Hackett, and confirmed ongoing platform innovation following the LeewayHertz acquisition.
  • Fernandez revealed that Hackett Group is finalizing a "global go-to-market collaboration agreement" with a global technology and consulting company and expects to launch a go-to-market pilot initiative with ServiceNow this month.
  • Rob Ramirez, CFO, stated, "For the fourth quarter of 2025, our total revenues before reimbursements were $74.8 million, which exceeded the high end of our guidance."
  • Ramirez commented, "We expect Q1 revenue to be up sequentially and gross margin to be up on a year-over-year basis and both to continue to increase throughout the year."

Outlook

  • The company estimates total revenues before reimbursements for the first quarter of 2026 to be in the range of $70.5 million to $72 million.
  • Adjusted diluted net income per common share in the first quarter of 2026 is projected to be in the range of $0.34 to $0.36.
  • Adjusted gross margin as a percentage of revenues before reimbursements is expected to be approximately 44% to 45%.
  • The company anticipates AI transition charges in Q1 of approximately $1 million to $1.5 million, primarily related to severance costs from headcount reductions.
  • Ramirez indicated, "We expect both Global S&BT and Oracle Solutions segments to be down when compared to the prior year, but sequentially up from Q4. We expect SAP Solutions segment revenue before reimbursements to continue to be up on a year-over-year basis."

Financial Results

  • Total revenues before reimbursements were $74.8 million for Q4 2025.
  • Global S&BT segment revenues were $38.6 million, Oracle Solutions $14 million, and SAP Solutions $22.2 million.
  • Approximately 22% of total company revenues before reimbursements consist of recurring multiyear and subscription-based revenues.
  • Adjusted cost of sales totaled $40 million, or 53.4% of revenues before reimbursements.
  • Consultant headcount was 1,301 at quarter-end.
  • Adjusted gross margin was 46.6% and adjusted SG&A was $20 million.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $15.9 million, and GAAP net income was $5.6 million or $0.21 per diluted share.
  • Adjusted net income was $10.9 million or $0.40 per share.
  • Cash balances ended at $18.2 million, with net cash from operations at $19.1 million.
  • The company completed a stock tender offer, repurchasing 2.1 million shares at an average of $20.30 per share, for a total cost of approximately $42 million.
  • Outstanding debt was $76 million at quarter-end.

Q&A

  • George Sutton, Craig-Hallum, asked about the outcome sought from a potential global partnership. Fernandez responded, "the capabilities of AI XPLR are really distinct...Our ability to first to simulate an entire industry's AI opportunities across 26 industries and our ability to do so with AI XPLR are distinct."
  • Sutton inquired about the ServiceNow go-to-market plan. Fernandez explained, "it's a pilot to target...the hope is to be able to launch that before the end of the month."
  • Sutton questioned transition costs from GenAI and resulting headcount reductions. Fernandez stated, "we're seeing productivity improvements that could result in some numbers that will be in excess of 25%."
  • Jeff Martin, ROTH Capital, asked about licensing progress. Fernandez indicated, "the product has been utilized in the version 4 capabilities...now if they would like to continue...the client has the option to license either one or both depending on how they want to avail themselves to that capability."
  • Martin followed up on outcome-based quoting. Fernandez said, "we evaluate each job and respond to the client requirements. And we quote the completion of the pass that the client has either in an RFP or whatever the detailed request is, and we quote that fee."
  • Vincent Colicchio, Barrington Research, requested an update on S&BT business trends. Fernandez replied, "Clearly, the number of clients that have an AI element is going to be increasing meaningfully throughout the year...OneStream, well, I mean, that group is up sequentially. S&BT, as Rob said, is expected to be up sequentially from Q4 to Q1."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was probing but constructive, centering on partnerships, platform productivity, cost structure shifts, and licensing progress.
  • Management displayed confidence, with Fernandez repeatedly emphasizing the distinctiveness and market relevance of AI XPLR and new platforms. Quotes such as "we have extreme capability, which continues to be very distinct" and "we are not making our platform available to clients for free. Let's make sure that's clear" reflect assertiveness.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone shifted from cautious optimism in awaiting licensing and partnership deals to increased confidence as new platforms are now launched and licensing is underway.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue before reimbursements rose from $72.2 million in Q3 to $74.8 million in Q4.
  • SAP Solutions segment revenue surged from $13.4 million in Q3 to $22.2 million in Q4, driven by strong software-related sales and S/4HANA cloud migrations.
  • Oracle Solutions continued to decline year-over-year but is expected to improve sequentially in Q1 2026.
  • Adjusted earnings per share rose from $0.37 to $0.40.
  • Management's tone has become more confident as the company transitions from developing and piloting GenAI platforms in Q3 to executing licensing and partnership initiatives in Q4.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on partnerships, platform differentiation, and revenue mix shifts, but the current quarter saw heightened interest in the tangible impacts of new platforms and cost structure changes.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited demand volatility and client confusion over rapidly evolving GenAI technology as ongoing headwinds.
  • The company anticipates continued AI transition charges related to headcount reductions and the leverage of AI delivery platforms.
  • Fernandez acknowledged, "demand for digital transformation remains solid in traditional areas, it continues to be impacted by thoughtful decision-making as organizations assess competing priorities partly due to economic concerns and also partly due to the consideration and also confusion of emerging GenAI technologies and what they offer."
  • Analyst questions reflected concern over the sustainability and client adoption of new platforms, as well as the pace of licensing growth and partnership execution.

Final Takeaway

The Hackett Group concluded the quarter by emphasizing the launch and licensability of its full GenAI platform suite, with a clear focus on expanding recurring revenues, improving productivity, and securing major partnerships to drive growth. Management projects sequential revenue and margin improvement into Q1 2026, while highlighting both the cost efficiencies and new revenue opportunities generated by its GenAI transition. The company remains committed to leveraging its proprietary platforms and IP, targeting further expansion through alliances and product innovation as it navigates market volatility and evolving client priorities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Polestar sets plans to launch four new models by 2028
data.description:
Polestar Automotive Holding UK (PSNY) is launching what it says is the largest product offensive in its history. The automaker outlined plans to introduce four new electric models over the next three years as it targets the core of the global electric vehicle market and pushes for profitable growth. 

By 2028, the Gothenburg, Sweden-based company plans to add a flagship Polestar 5 four-door grand tourer, a new variant of the Polestar 4, a next-generation Polestar 2 sedan, and a new Polestar 7 compact SUV. The Polestar 5, built on a lightweight bonded aluminum platform, is slated for deliveries from summer 2026 and is positioned as a halo car to lift the brand’s performance and luxury credentials. A more versatile Polestar 4 variant aimed at a wider customer base is due to launch later in 2026, combining estate-like space and SUV versatility to reinforce the company’s best-selling nameplate.

Polestar will also replace its original volume model with a "completely new" Polestar 2 successor in early 2027, aiming to leverage an installed base of more than 190K cars and an active owner community. In 2028, the planned Polestar 7 compact premium SUV will push the brand into what it describes as Europe’s largest EV segment, with production to be based in Europe and pricing positioned to broaden its addressable market.

"With our attractive model lineup, strong shareholder support, and partners with access to the latest technology, Polestar is set to become one of the winners of the automotive industry’s transformation," stated Polestar (PSNY) CEO Michael Lohscheller.

Notably, the Swedish brand expects low double-digit retail volume growth in 2026 and a 30% expansion of its retail network, helped by a shifting sales mix toward its Polestar 4 crossover. Financial guidance tied to the expansion will be detailed with the company’s 2025 full-year earnings report.

Shares of Polestar Automotive (PSNY) are down more than 20% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Mamdani pitches NYC property tax hike to close budget gap
data.description:
New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has pitched hiking property taxes by 9.5% as "a last resort" to help close an estimated $5.4B two-year revenue shortfall, if his proposal to tax the rich falls through.

Mamdani proposed raising personal income taxes on New Yorkers earning more than $1M annually and hiking taxes on the most profitable corporations, a path that he said is "the most sustainable and fairest" to bridge the city's budget gap.

But this would require the approval of the governor and state legislature, and New York Gov. Kathy Hochul has opposed this pitch.

Because of this, Mamdani proposed a "more harmful" path of raising property taxes and drawing $1.2B from the city's reserves to plug the budget deficit.

"We do not want to have to turn to such drastic measures to balance our budget," Mamdani said while announcing his $127B preliminary budget. "But, faced with no other choice, we will be forced to."

Hochul said she is not supportive of a property tax increase, but noted that the decision is up to the City Council and Mamdani. "That's their prerogative to look at that as an option."

Hochul, who is up for re-election this year, on Monday announced that the state will allocate an additional $1.5B over two years to help address New York City's fiscal challenges.

If the city's property taxes were raised, it would be the first such hike in more than two decades. And this would likely lead to higher rents, as landlords pass through the costs to tenants, and may push businesses away from the city.

New York City Council Speaker Julie Menin and Council Member Linda Lee said there are "additional areas of savings and revenue that deserve careful scrutiny before increasing the burden on small property owners and neighborhood small businesses, which could worsen the affordability crisis."

Mark Levine, the city's comptroller, said the proposed property tax hike would be regressive, and drawing down reserves would leave New York City vulnerable to economic turbulence next year.

New York-exposed REITs include SL Green Realty (SLG), Vornado Realty Trust (VNO), Equity Residential (EQR), Empire State Realty Trust (ESRT) and LXP Industrial Trust (LXP).

data.title: Ternium GAAP EPADS of $0.62 misses by $0.28, revenue of $3.77B misses by $10M
data.description:
• Ternium press release (TX): Q4 GAAP EPADS of $0.62 misses by $0.28.
  • Revenue of $3.77B (-2.8% Y/Y) misses by $10M.
  • In the fourth quarter of 2025, Adjusted EBITDA had a slight sequential decrease to $395 million. 
  • Ternium's Net Cash position stood stable at $712 million at the end of December 2025, underpinned by operating cash flow of $528 million and a $204 million increase in the market value of financial securities.

  • Outlook

    Ternium anticipates an increase in Adjusted EBITDA in the first quarter of 2026 compared to the fourth quarter of 2025. Shipments are expected to rise, primarily in Mexico. Adjusted EBITDA margin is also expected to improve, driven by an increase in revenue per ton, in both Mexico and Brazil, partially offset by higher costs per ton.

data.title: Food service companies are expected to benefit on the bottom line from agentic AI
data.description:
Wells Fargo thinks food service companies look well positioned to capture AI-related upside because the complex, low-margin logistics AI models could lead to material supply chain efficiencies and accelerated share gain. The firm pointed to a growing “AI winner” narrative regarding the group.

"We see significant opportunity in food service, as it looks well positioned to capture cost deflation from AI efficiency. The industry is logistics heavy, with private fleets, dense routes, large warehouse networks, high-cost labor, complex SKUs, and time-sensitive deliveries," highlighted analyst Edward Kelley.

It was noted that distributors have thin margins, so even incremental productivity gains matter. The Wells Fargo analyst team estimates supply chain costs represent 60% of operating expenses, and each 1% savings yields about 3% to EPS. Importantly, large distributors control large private fleets, complex warehouse networks, and proprietary ordering platforms, allowing them to internalize AI benefits across the system. The potential for supply chain cost savings, accelerated share gains, better earnings growth, and more durable cash flows is expected to raise the valuation ceiling for the group.

Wells Fargo has Overweight ratings on Sysco (SYY), Performance Food Group (PFGC), and US Foods (USFD).

data.title: Enpro Non-GAAP EPS of $1.99 beats by $0.08, revenue of $295.4M beats by $14.43M
data.description:
• Enpro press release (NPO): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.99 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $295.4M (+14.3% Y/Y) beats by $14.43M.
  • Sales of $295.4 million increased 14.3%. Strength in aerospace, food and biopharma, and domestic general industrial markets, in addition to some recovery in semiconductor demand and strategic pricing actions, offset continued weakness in commercial vehicle OEM demand, a choppy nuclear power market in Europe, and slow general industrial sales internationally. Excluding the acquisition contributions and foreign currency exchange translation, organic sales grew 9.9%.

  • 2026 Guidance

    • Introducing guidance for 2026: Total revenue growth in the 8% to 12% range vs consensus of 20.62%, adjusted EBITDA in the range of $305 million to $320 million and adjusted diluted earnings per share of $8.50 to $9.20
    • Strong balance sheet and free cash flow generation provide financial flexibility to further organic growth initiatives and select strategic acquisitions

data.title: Markets AM Need to Know: Microsoft's AI expansion, Meta legal challenge, and more
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher before the bell as Wall Street looked toward a positive open with technology stocks regaining momentum.

Now, here are 5 news stories that broke overnight to watch out for:

Critical minerals policy: The U.S. has developed a critical minerals price floor system that it is pitching to more than 50 countries as the Trump administration seeks to reduce dependence on China for resources deemed critical to national security. Under Secretary of State for Economic Affairs Jacob Helberg said multiple U.S. agencies have developed the system and are having conversations with allies and partners about the initiative.

Microsoft AI expansion: Microsoft (MSFT) plans to invest $50 billion by the end of the decade to expand AI access across countries in the Global South. The tech giant announced the commitment at the India AI Impact Summit, stating the funding will support wider adoption of artificial intelligence and address the growing divide in AI capabilities between developed and emerging economies.

S&P 500 outlook: After briefly climbing above 7,000 earlier in 2026, the S&P 500 (SP500) has retreated and is now hovering near 6,836. Prediction market Kalshi shows traders pricing a 66% chance of the index reaching 7,200 or higher, while 7,400 or above carries a 48% probability. Odds taper significantly for projections above 7,600.

Meta legal challenge: Mark Zuckerberg will testify this week in a landmark trial alleging that Meta’s (META) social media platforms deliberately addict and harm children. The case was brought by a now 20-year-old woman, identified by the initials KGM, who claims early social media use worsened her depression and suicidal thoughts.

Nvidia-Meta partnership: Nvidia (NVDA) announced a wide-ranging, multi-year partnership with Meta (META) that will see the social media company use several Nvidia products to boost its artificial intelligence infrastructure. Meta is increasing its usage of Nvidia’s Grace CPUs in its data centers, marking the first large-scale Grace-only deployment.

data.title: Costco stuns with its Nike sneaker drop that was popular with Generation X and Z
data.description:
Costco Wholesale Corporation (COST) created viral buzz this month with a shocking in-store-only drop of a Kirkland-branded Nike (NKE) shoe. While the shop drop has ended, resale prices on sneakerhead sites show that interest is still sky-high with prices over $600 for some transactions.

The Nike SB Dunk Low x Kirkland Signature was a limited collaboration sneaker that was quietly released through select Costco (COST) warehouses. The shoe line appeared without advance notice at a small number of warehouses in states including New York, Oregon, California, and Washington, and some additional locations as word spread. The $135 shoe was generally restricted to one pair per Costco (COST) member, reinforcing the sense of exclusivity and driving quick sell-through.

 

 

 

Designed on the Nike SB Dunk Low silhouette, the sneaker features a grey fleece or sweatshirt-style upper that mimics cozy Kirkland loungewear, with prominent Kirkland Signature branding on the heel and printed on the insoles. Additional details include a tongue label styled like a Costco price tag and insole graphics that reference Costco's (COST) famous $1.50 hot dog combo. Interestingly, reports indicate the shoe was popular with both Generation X for its retro vibe and Generation Z as a social media phenomenon.

Neither Nike (NKE) nor Costco (COST) has publicly confirmed final production numbers, restock plans, or the full door list of where their shoes were sold. However, analysts see the collaboration potentially extending with more products.
